title: NextEra Energy (NEE)Intrinsic Value
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on NextEra Energy using FY2025 actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what NEE is really worth.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-o7c3nvrh>
A very high phase 1 growth rate is used because FY2025 FCF ($3.76B) is artificially low relative to the company's earning power, as capital expenditures remain elevated. As CapEx moderates and recent investments in wind, solar, and storage come online and generate operating cash flow, FCF will expand rapidly on a percentage basis.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-o7c3nvrh>
A very high phase 1 growth rate is used because FY2025 FCF ($3.76B) is artificially low relative to the company's earning power, as capital expenditures remain elevated. As CapEx moderates and recent investments in wind, solar, and storage come online and generate operating cash flow, FCF will expand rapidly on a percentage basis.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-o7c3nvrh>
Utilities generally track population growth, electricity demand growth, and inflation. 2.5% is a standard, conservative long-term growth estimate for a premier utility operating in structurally growing markets like Florida.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use a 6% discount rate? Isn't that too low?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "NextEra Energy (NEE) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 18, 2026.
